file.content:
# Review: `deepmind/593.lean` — Erdős Problem 593

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports` at line 17.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line (line 19).
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 593`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link: `[erdosproblems.com/593](https://www.erdosproblems.com/593)`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (characterize obligatory finite 3-uniform hypergraphs; conjectures they are the 2-colorable ones).
- [x] Bibliography entries present: `[EGH75]` and `[Er95d]`.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos593 ... end Erdos593` (lines 42–91).
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos593`.
- [x] No `open` / `open scoped` statements needed; none present. N/A.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] All helper definitions have LaTeX docstrings (`/-- ... -/`):
  - `Hypergraph3` (line 44): describes a 3-uniform hypergraph.
  - `Hypergraph3.IsProperColoring` (line 50): proper coloring definition.
  - `Hypergraph3.HasUncountableChromaticNumber` (line 56): uncountable chromatic number.
  - `Hypergraph3.ContainsCopy` (line 62): subhypergraph embedding.
  - `Hypergraph3.Is2Colorable` (line 68): 2-colorability.
- [x] No definitions involve classical choice; `noncomputable` not needed.
- [x] No `sorry` in any definition.
- [x] Types and Props use `UpperCamelCase`: `Hypergraph3`, `IsProperColoring`, `HasUncountableChromaticNumber`, `ContainsCopy`, `Is2Colorable`.
- [x] No problem-specific notation needed; `local notation` not required. N/A.
- [ ] No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks for definitions. Optional but would help verify the definitions behave as expected (e.g., a small example hypergraph that is/isn't 2-colorable).

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword (line 84).
- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_593` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` conventions.
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same** attribute line: `@[category research open, AMS 5]` (line 83).
- [x] Has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`, lines 73–82).
- [x] Docstring references source papers: `[Er95d]`, `[EGH75]`.

### `answer()` usage

The original problem says "Characterize those finite 3-uniform hypergraphs which appear in every 3-uniform hypergraph of chromatic number > ℵ₀." This could be read as Case 2 ("determine which ones"), but the formalization conjectures a specific characterization (2-colorability) and states it as a direct biconditional assertion. This is **Case 3** — a direct assertion — and correctly uses no `answer()` wrapper.

- [x] Theorem type is `P` directly (a biconditional `↔`), with no `answer()` — appropriate for a direct assertion (Case 3).
- [x] No quantifiers incorrectly placed as theorem parameters for an `answer()` statement — `answer()` not used, so N/A.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]`: `category research open`.
- [x] Valid category value: `research open`.
- [x] `research open` theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body (line 89).

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 5`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `5` (Combinatorics).
- [x] Numbers in ascending order: single number, trivially satisfied.
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] `5` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code. Appropriate for a problem about hypergraph chromatic numbers.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Open conjecture uses `by sorry` (multi-line form: `by` on line 88, `sorry` on line 89 — acceptable).
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for this open research problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A — no variants present. The problem is a single characterization conjecture.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `∀`, `∃`, `∈`, `¬`, `↔`, `→`, `↪`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not run in this review. Must be verified separately.
- [ ] Custom linter pass status not verified. Must be verified separately.

---

## Summary

**Result: PASS** (modulo build verification)

The file is clean and well-structured. Five custom definitions (`Hypergraph3`, `IsProperColoring`, `HasUncountableChromaticNumber`, `ContainsCopy`, `Is2Colorable`) are all properly documented with LaTeX docstrings. The main theorem `erdos_593` correctly formalizes the conjecture as a biconditional (Case 3, no `answer()` needed) with proper category and AMS attributes. No issues found in formatting, naming, or style.

**Minor suggestion:** Consider adding `@[category test]` sanity-check examples to verify the definitions (e.g., a small hypergraph that is/isn't 2-colorable). This is optional per the checklist.

content.text: The review passed (**Result: PASS**). No changes needed.
